_recipe: A/B-over-path benchmark — Open Shaders example. Runs a recorded camera path TWICE, once per A/B variant, so both variants are measured at the SAME positions (vs. the timer rotation that flips mid-path). POST this body to /api/tool/scenario (the scenario runner dispatches each step). Requires a consumer that registered an 'openshaders.abtest' tool (Open Shaders) with a TEST config authored in its Performance Overlay, and a recording from record/stop. Replace <RECORDING> with that file path. Read the 'results' step's output for the per-draw-call A/B timing.
_notes: pass-A restores the scene (loads the recording's entry save, then the local settle delay); pass-B does NOT restore (stays in the same scene so positions match). The abtest switch between passes is manual so swaps align to whole passes. 'clear' resets any prior aggregation; 'stop' restores the TEST config.
